name: Enoxaparin
executiveSummary: Anticoagulant. Prophylaxis and treatment of deep vein thrombosis and/or pulmonary thromboembolism and arterial thrombosis.
indications:
## Main indications

Anticoagulant. Prophylaxis and treatment of deep vein thrombosis and/or pulmonary thromboembolism and arterial thrombosis.

> Adjust according to institutional protocol and medical prescription.

adulto.dose: Prophylaxis: 40 mg SC every 24 h. PE: 1 mg/kg SC every 12 h.
adulto.administration: Deep SC; do not rub injection site.

pediatrico.dose: Per specialist indication. Prophylactic dose: < 2 months: 0,75 mg/kg/dose every 12 h; > 2 months: 0,5 mg/kg/dose every 12 h. Therapeutic dose: < 2 months: 1,5 mg/kg/dose every 12 h; > 2 months: 1 mg/kg/dose every 12 h
pediatrico.presentation: Prefilled syringe: 20 mg – 40 mg – 60 mg – 80 mg – 100 mg
pediatrico.notes: Lower bleeding risk than heparin, prolonged half-life, less laboratory monitoring. If combination with substances affecting hemostasis is indicated, perform careful clinical and laboratory monitoring. See alert.

neonatal.dose: 1,5 mg/kg SC every 12 h in NICU (protocol).

stability:
## Stability

- Prefilled syringes ready for use.
adverseEffects:
## Adverse effects

Thrombocytopenia (rare), hypochromic anemia, cutaneous necrosis at injection site, hemorrhage, fever, diarrhea, confusion, dyspnea, edema.
